title: phlox longifolia subsp. brevifolia
intro: Phlox longifolia subsp. brevifolia, also known as mountain phlox, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of the western United States, characterized by its pink or purple flowers and lance-shaped leaves. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of mountain phlox include relieving coughs and colds, reducing inflammation and pain, and promoting respiratory health. Mountain phlox can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate specific health issues.
